diagnoses.name: Acute pulmonary edema
diagnoses.evidence_span: Acute decompensated heart failure with pulmonary edema

diagnoses.name: Atherosclerotic heart disease of native coronary artery
diagnoses.evidence_span: significant cardiac history including atherosclerotic heart disease of native coronary arteries

diagnoses.name: Ischemic cardiomyopathy
diagnoses.evidence_span: ischemic cardiomyopathy, though without active anginal symptoms at presentation

diagnoses.name: Hypoxemia
diagnoses.evidence_span: Upon arrival, he demonstrated significant hypoxemia requiring supplemental oxygen

diagnoses.name: New-onset atrial fibrillation
diagnoses.evidence_span: During the hospital course, the patient experienced new-onset atrial fibrillation which was treated with antiarrhythmic therapy

diagnoses.name: Acute posthemorrhagic anemia
diagnoses.evidence_span: the pt sustained acute posthemorrhagic anemia during hospitalization, likely related to procedural blood loss

diagnoses.name: Ulcerative colitis
diagnoses.evidence_span: The patient has a remote history of ulcerative colitis, now managed via colostomy status following previous surgical intervention

diagnoses.name: Colostomy status
diagnoses.evidence_span: now managed via colostomy status following previous surgical intervention

diagnoses.name: Type 2 diabetes mellitus
diagnoses.evidence_span: His metabolic comorbidities include type 2 diabetes mellitus and hyperlipidemia, both controlled on current medical regimens

diagnoses.name: Hyperlipidemia
diagnoses.evidence_span: His metabolic comorbidities include type 2 diabetes mellitus and hyperlipidemia, both controlled on current medical regimens

diagnoses.name: Chronic insomnia
diagnoses.evidence_span: The patient also reports chronic insomnia

procedures.name: Cardiac output monitoring with continuous assessment
procedures.evidence_span: The pt underwent cardiac output monitoring with continuous assessment throughout admission

procedures.name: Coronary artery bypass grafting
procedures.evidence_span: he proceeded to coronary artery bypass grafting via open approach. Specifically, the patient had bypass to three coronary arteries from the aorta using autologous venous tissue, with additional bypass to one coronary artery from the left internal mammary artery

procedures.name: Left saphenous vein harvest via percutaneous endoscopic approach
procedures.evidence_span: The left saphenous vein was harvested via percutaneous endoscopic approach for graft material

medications.evidence_span: Aspirin 81 mg PO daily

medications.name: Aspirin EC
medications.evidence_span: Aspirin EC 81 mg PO daily

medications.name: Atorvastatin
medications.evidence_span: Atorvastatin 80 mg PO daily

medications.name: Carvedilol
medications.evidence_span: Carvedilol 6.25 mg PO BID (and 3.125 mg PO dosing available)

medications.name: Lisinopril
medications.evidence_span: Lisinopril 2.5 mg PO daily

medications.name: Metoprolol Tartrate
medications.evidence_span: Metoprolol Tartrate 12.5 mg PO BID (with 5-6.25 mg dosing available)

medications.name: Amiodarone
medications.evidence_span: Amiodarone 400 mg PO daily with 600 mg IV drip as needed

medications.name: Furosemide
medications.evidence_span: Furosemide 20 mg PO/IV daily

medications.name: Metformin
medications.evidence_span: Metformin 500 mg PO BID (with 250 mg dosing available)

medications.name: Insulin
medications.evidence_span: Insulin per CVICU protocol subcutaneously as directed

medications.evidence_span: Albuterol and ipratropium nebulizers as needed

medications.evidence_span: Albuterol and ipratropium nebulizers as needed

medications.name: Ranitidine

medications.name: Tramadol

medications.name: Oxycodone

medications.name: Alprazolam
medications.evidence_span: Alprazolam 0.5 mg PO daily as needed for anxiety

medications.evidence_span: propofol for sedation

medications.name: Normal saline
medications.evidence_span: Hemodynamic support required both crystalloid and colloid administration, including normal saline, lactated Ringer's solution

medications.name: Lactated Ringer's solution
medications.evidence_span: including normal saline, lactated Ringer's solution, and multiple albumin infusions

medications.name: Albumin
medications.evidence_span: multiple albumin infusions at various concentrations

medications.name: Potassium chloride
medications.evidence_span: Electrolyte management was complex, requiring repeated potassium chloride supplementation via multiple routes (oral, intravenous, and phosphate-based)

medications.name: Calcium gluconate
medications.evidence_span: calcium gluconate, and magnesium sulfate administration

medications.name: Magnesium sulfate
medications.evidence_span: calcium gluconate, and magnesium sulfate administration

medications.name: Insulin drips
medications.evidence_span: The patient required insulin drips and subcutaneous insulin dosing per CVICU protocol

medications.name: Dextrose
medications.evidence_span: point-of-care glucose management with dextrose supplementation when hypoglycemia was detected

medications.name: Cefazolin
medications.evidence_span: Antibiotic prophylaxis included cefazolin

medications.name: Chlorhexidine
medications.evidence_span: chlorhexidine oral rinses

medications.name: Metoclopramide
medications.evidence_span: Antiemetic coverage was maintained with metoclopramide and ondansetron

medications.name: Simethicone
medications.evidence_span: Stool softening agents included docusate sodium and simethicone

medications.name: Fentanyl
medications.evidence_span: Pain and anxiety management required tramadol, acetaminophen (via multiple routes including intravenous, oral, and rectal), oxycodone, and fentanyl patches

chief_complaint: Acute decompensated heart failure with pulmonary edema
